top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The day is dominated by a fast-moving Ebola outbreak in Central Africa, which the WHO has just upgraded to a global health emergency, and the slow thaw in US-China trade tensions after Beijing said Washington has agreed not to push tariffs higher. Bolivia is on the brink as protests strangle La Paz, and Putin's Beijing visit underlines the Russia-China axis. Domestically, the US is grappling with the aftermath of a deadly mosque shooting in San Diego.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a global health emergency
stories.summary: The WHO Director-General determined on May 17 that the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak constitutes a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. As of May 20, around 600 suspected cases and 139 deaths have been logged, with 51 lab-confirmed cases in DRC and two imported cases in Uganda. No approved vaccine exists for this strain.

stories.headline: Beijing says Washington has agreed not to raise tariffs above truce ceiling
stories.summary: China's commerce ministry says talks held in South Korea just before President Trump's Beijing visit produced a US commitment to cap future tariffs at the levels set in last year's truce. Both sides reportedly agreed in principle to discuss reciprocal cuts on at least $30 billion of goods, signalling a shift from shock tariffs to 'managed competition'.

stories.headline: Bolivia's capital under siege as protests deepen President Paz's crisis
stories.summary: Two weeks of road blockades by unions, miners and farmers have emptied La Paz markets and drained hospital oxygen reserves. Business groups estimate $50 million a day in losses, with at least three deaths after emergency vehicles were blocked. The trigger: a contested land-mortgage law amid fuel shortages and 20% inflation.

stories.headline: Putin opens talks with Xi in Beijing, calls Russia and China 'partners'
stories.summary: President Vladimir Putin praised his close personal ties with Xi Jinping as the two leaders began bilateral talks in Beijing. The trip cements the Moscow-Beijing alignment at a moment when Russia is taking heavier battlefield losses in Ukraine and faces tightening Western sanctions.

stories.headline: Three killed in shooting at San Diego mosque, probed as a hate crime
stories.summary: A gunman opened fire at the largest mosque complex in San Diego, killing three worshippers. Police say they are investigating the attack as a hate crime. The shooting has reignited calls for stronger protections at houses of worship across the US.

stories.headline: Air France flight diverts to Montreal under US Ebola entry restrictions
stories.summary: An Air France flight from Paris to Detroit was forced to divert to Montreal after US authorities imposed enhanced screening tied to the Central Africa Ebola outbreak. The CDC and DHS announced expanded entry rules on May 18 to keep the Bundibugyo strain out of the country.

stories.headline: Leaked memo: US threatened to revoke Palestinian visas over UN bid
stories.summary: A leaked State Department cable shows the US warned it would pull Palestinian diplomats' visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post. The disclosure has prompted criticism from European allies and put a fresh strain on already fragile Gaza diplomacy.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: It's a quietly active news day at home: the NEA is warning of more afternoon thunderstorms and a hot 33-35 degC stretch, while the May 2nd-round COE results pushed Cat B premiums above $129k - another record high for big cars. The CDA confirmed the two residents from the MV Hondius cruise tested negative for hantavirus but are still under a 30-day quarantine. Lighter news: Snow City will close after 26 years, and a parliament motion pledged 'no jobless growth' from AI.

stories.headline: NEA: Afternoon thundery showers expected on most days through end-May, highs up to 35 degC
stories.summary: The Meteorological Service Singapore says short-duration thundery showers will continue on most afternoons into late May, occasionally lasting into the evening. Sumatra squalls may bring gusty pre-dawn rain. Daytime highs are forecast at 33-34 degC, hitting 35 degC on a few days. Tampines logged 114.6 mm of rain on May 8, the highest in the first half of May.

stories.headline: COE premiums hit fresh highs in May's second bidding: Cat B closes at $129,501
stories.summary: The May 2nd-round Certificate of Entitlement results saw Cat A close at $124,229, Cat B at $129,501, Cat C at $92,223, Cat D at $9,689 and Cat E at $130,000. Cat B and Cat E reached new highs, keeping new-car ownership effectively out of reach for many households.

stories.headline: Two Singapore residents from MV Hondius cleared of hantavirus, quarantine continues
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed both Singapore residents who travelled on the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius tested negative at the National Public Health Laboratory on May 8. They remain in 30-day quarantine from last exposure, followed by 15 days of phone surveillance, as a precaution. Public risk is rated low.

stories.headline: Snow City to close on Sep 30 after 26 years
stories.summary: The indoor snow attraction at Jurong East will shut its doors on Sep 30, 2026 after 26 years. Operators cited rising maintenance costs and a shift in family entertainment habits. The Jurong site is being redeveloped as part of the wider Science Centre relocation.

stories.headline: Parliament passes labour movement motion pledging no jobless growth in AI era
stories.summary: Lawmakers unanimously backed a private member's motion - the first tabled by the labour movement in over a decade - affirming that Singapore's economic progress through the AI transition will be 'inclusive' with no jobless growth. NTUC has been pushing for re-skilling subsidies and stronger displacement protections.

stories.headline: Admiralty incident: police report filed after woman seen resting baby on block ledge
stories.summary: Footage circulating online showed a woman placing an 11-month-old baby on the ledge of an HDB block in Admiralty. The parents say their child's behaviour has changed noticeably since the incident, and a police report has been filed. Authorities are investigating.

stories.headline: PHV driver stays four hours at Lim Chu Kang cemetery to help passengers tend graves
stories.summary: A 39-year-old private-hire driver waited with passengers at Lim Chu Kang cemetery for around four hours, even helping clean and tend to their relatives' graves while they paid respects. The story has gone viral on local social media as a feel-good moment.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three active theatres are grinding rather than tipping. Russia is taking record losses near Pokrovsk while Ukraine inches forward in Kupyansk and western Zaporizhia. The US-Iran ceasefire holds in name but the Strait of Hormuz remains essentially closed, with global shipping rerouted. In Lebanon, a 45-day truce extension hasn't stopped near-daily Israeli strikes, and Gaza diplomacy is stalled over the post-war 'Board of Peace' framework.

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine across multiple axes.
Ukraine's Kharkiv counter-offensive recaptures large parts of the north-east.
Ukrainian summer counter-offensive in the south makes only incremental gains.
Ukraine launches surprise cross-border incursion into Russia's Kursk region.
Trump-Putin direct talks begin; partial Black Sea grain understanding holds.
Russia loses more territory than it captures for the first time in nearly two years.
Russian strikes kill 22 in Ukraine on the eve of an announced ceasefire window.
ISW: Russian forces advance in Sumy and Pokrovsk; Ukraine regains ground in Kupyansk and western Zaporizhia.

Ukraine's General Staff reports 177 combat clashes in 24 hours; cumulative Russian losses estimated at ~1.35 million personnel. Russia launches 1 ballistic missile and 238 drones overnight and into the day.

theatres.name: US/Israel - Iran (Strait of Hormuz Crisis)
theatres.status: Ceasefire in name, Strait still closed

timeline.event: US and Israel launch joint air war on Iran; Supreme Leader Khamenei assassinated.
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' and threatens any ship that attempts to transit.
Commercial traffic through the Strait drops more than 90%; Asia faces fuel shortages.
US and Iran reach a ceasefire that also covers Israel after five weeks of fighting.
IMO: ~20,000 mariners and ~2,000 ships stranded in the Persian Gulf.
UAE intercepts incoming missiles; drone sparks fire at an Emirati oil site.
US declares the offensive phase over; Tehran insists it will not let Washington control Hormuz.
Hormuz remains effectively closed; brinkmanship between Washington and Tehran continues over reopening terms.

theatres.name: Israel - Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Truce extended but daily strikes continue

timeline.event: Hamas attack on southern Israel triggers war in Gaza; Hezbollah opens northern front.
First Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire reached, then repeatedly violated.
Israel launches a new ground operation into southern Lebanon against Hezbollah.
Strikes in southern Lebanon kill at least 22 people, including 8 children, in a single day.
Israel-Lebanon truce extended for another 45 days; airstrikes continue.
Times of Israel reports a captured Hamas document detailing fighter training during the early-2025 Gaza ceasefire.
Lebanese Health Ministry: cumulative toll since March now 3,073 killed. Gaza talks stall on the post-war 'Board of Peace' framework; Hamas demands full ceasefire and IDF withdrawal.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak appears to be plateauing but is not over - the ship is now docked in Rotterdam being sanitised, and former passengers are scattered across at least 12 countries. The latest official figures are 11 cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths. The two Singapore residents on board tested negative but remain in precautionary quarantine. The bigger story is that hantavirus has joined Ebola in stretching global health surveillance systems this month.

stories.headline: ECDC update: 11 Andes hantavirus cases tied to MV Hondius, 3 deaths
stories.summary: The European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control's May 21 update raises the cluster linked to the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius to 11 cases - 9 confirmed and 2 probable - with three deaths, two of them lab-confirmed as hantavirus. The ECDC still rates the risk to the wider EU/EEA population as 'very low' given how Andes hantavirus spreads.

stories.headline: MV Hondius now docked in Rotterdam, undergoing deep sanitisation
stories.summary: The MV Hondius has been docked in Rotterdam since May 18 for ship-wide decontamination. Passengers and crew came from 23 countries; former travellers are currently hospitalised or quarantined in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey and the United States.

stories.headline: Singapore CDA: two residents tested negative, still in 30-day quarantine
stories.summary: Singapore's Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed on May 8 that both Singapore residents who were on the MV Hondius tested negative for hantavirus, including Andes virus, at the National Public Health Laboratory. They remain in a 30-day precautionary quarantine from the date of last exposure, with further phone surveillance to follow.

stories.headline: CDC: enhanced traveller screening continues for returning Hondius passengers
stories.summary: The US CDC says symptomatic screening, contact-tracing and follow-up monitoring continue for returning MV Hondius passengers. Andes hantavirus is unusual in that limited person-to-person transmission has been documented in past South American outbreaks, prompting the cautious response.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Two themes dominate this cycle: actively exploited zero-days that Microsoft and Cisco only patched after attacks were already underway, and the first wave of AI-generated exploit code Google says it caught in the wild. Defenders should prioritise the Cisco SD-WAN flaw (CVSS 10.0) and the new Exchange XSS on the CISA KEV list, both with imminent federal patch deadlines. On the breach front, the ShinyHunters claim of 275 million records pulled from Canvas would be one of the largest education-sector compromises ever, if confirmed.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN auth-bypass exploited as zero-day - CVSS 10.0
stories.summary: Cisco confirms attackers are actively exploiting a critical authentication bypass in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller (CVE-2026-20182) to gain administrative privileges on managed devices. CISA added the bug to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with a federal patch deadline of May 17, 2026.

stories.headline: CISA adds Microsoft Exchange XSS to KEV, agencies must patch by May 29
stories.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-42897, a Microsoft Exchange Server cross-site scripting flaw, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after seeing it abused in the wild. The bug lets attackers run arbitrary JavaScript inside Outlook Web Access in the victim's browser context. Federal agencies must patch by May 29.

stories.headline: NGINX 'Rift' - 18-year-old heap overflow lets attackers run code unauthenticated
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed a heap buffer overflow in NGINX's ngx_http_rewrite_module that has been in every build since 2008. Dubbed 'NGINX Rift', the flaw lets an unauthenticated attacker send a crafted HTTP request and gain remote code execution. Patches are out; admins should treat this as urgent given how widely NGINX is deployed.

stories.headline: Linux local privilege escalation flaw added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-31431, a local privilege escalation flaw across multiple Linux distributions, to its KEV catalog after evidence of active in-the-wild abuse. An unprivileged local user can chain the bug to obtain root. Patches are available from major distros.

stories.headline: Windows DNS Client RCE - attacker-controlled responses trigger remote code execution
stories.summary: Among the 120 fixes in May's Patch Tuesday, CVE-2026-41096 stands out: a malicious DNS server can send a crafted response to a vulnerable Windows client and run code. No active exploitation has been seen yet, but the attack chain is straightforward.

stories.headline: ASP.NET Core data-protection flaw allows SYSTEM via forged cookies
stories.summary: Microsoft pushed an out-of-band patch for CVE-2026-40372 in the ASP.NET Core Data Protection cryptographic APIs. An unauthenticated attacker who can reach a vulnerable app may forge authentication cookies and execute code with SYSTEM privileges on the host.

stories.headline: Google: first AI-generated zero-day for 2FA bypass thwarted in the wild
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group says it caught a cybercrime group using AI to build a zero-day designed to bypass two-factor authentication in an open-source admin tool, intended for a mass-exploitation campaign. Google's discovery and coordinated disclosure appear to have prevented the attack from going live.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters claim Canvas breach exposing 275 million education records
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters ransomware group says it stole roughly 275 million records from the Canvas learning management platform, covering students, teachers and staff across 8,809 school districts, universities and online education providers. If verified, it would be one of the largest education-sector breaches on record.

stories.headline: GitHub supply-chain breach: malicious Nx Console VS Code extension compromises 3,800 repos
stories.summary: A malicious version of the popular Nx Console VS Code extension, distributed through the TanStack npm supply chain attack, gave attackers access to roughly 3,800 internal GitHub repositories. GitHub has revoked tokens and is notifying impacted orgs.

stories.headline: MiniPlasma Windows 0-day enables SYSTEM on fully-patched Windows 11
stories.summary: Researcher Will Dormann disclosed 'MiniPlasma', a revived 2020 Windows cldflt.sys flaw that 'reliably' opens a SYSTEM-level cmd.exe on Windows 11 systems running the May 2026 cumulative update. Microsoft has been notified; no patch yet.
